title: Third-Party Actions, Pinning, Dependency Trust, and Workflow CodeQL · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Workflow Change Ownership Protection
summary: A workflow uses mutable tags from unknown publishers, executes a transitive action update without review, allows untrusted workflow edits, and has no inventory or emergency revocation path. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci workflow change ownership protection increment in action dependency and update policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Protect workflow and action code with CODEOWNERS, required review, branch rules, environment separation, and constrained bypass.
Inventory direct, reusable, composite, Docker, and nested action dependencies plus their effective privilege and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Ci Workflow Change Ownership Protection · Theory Ci Workflow Change Ownership Protection
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory release uses mutable action tags, accepts automated updates without runtime review, and makes an SLSA claim from incomplete provenance. Pin immutable identities, review diffs, verify attestations, and state non-guarantees. Competency probe: Protect workflow and action code with CODEOWNERS, required review, branch rules, environment separation, and constrained bypass.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ci Workflow Change Ownership Protection contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Ci Workflow Change Ownership Protection · Theory Ci Workflow Change Ownership Protection.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for protect workflow and action code with codeowners, required review, branch rules, environment separation, and constrained bypass. For Predict Ci Workflow Change Ownership Protection · Theory Ci Workflow Change Ownership Protection, keep Ci Workflow Change Ownership Protection connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name event, workflow, revision, actor, permissions, runner, toolchain, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, user-signal, and recovery boundaries; predict every state and trust transition; review the smallest deterministic workflow or release change; then verify a fault, repair, rollback, and authorized live-system transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release uses mutable action tags, accepts automated updates without runtime review, and makes an SLSA claim from incomplete provenance. Pin immutable identities, review diffs, verify attestations, and state non-guarantees. Competency probe: Protect workflow and action code with CODEOWNERS, required review, branch rules, environment separation, and constrained bypass. A defensible solution applies Ci Workflow Change Ownership Protection, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to action dependency and update policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-gha-actions-dependencies-supply-chain-4
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-actions-dependencies-supply-chain-4
    artifact: action-dependency-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha actions dependencies supply chain 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      allowed-actions: reviewed-publishers
      third-party-pin: full-commit-sha
      version-comment: retained
      nested-inventory: generated
      effective-permissions: reviewed
      workflow-codeowners: delivery-security
      update: automated-pr-with-compat-tests
      analysis: codeql-actions-and-policy
      revocation: rehearsed
    verify: enumerate nested actions resolve immutable SHAs inspect bundled runtime inputs post steps permissions advisories and update evidence
    failure: Reject marketplace trust, mutable major tags, top-level-only inventory, general review assumptions, or static analysis as complete proof.
    repair: Review effective code and authority, pin immutable revisions, protect workflow changes, test updates, enforce policy, and rehearse removal.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: General code review rules always apply equally to workflow-file changes. This fails the Ci Workflow Change Ownership Protection contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Protect workflow and action code with CODEOWNERS, required review, branch rules, environment separation, and constrained bypass.
